title: ISE Authorization-Policy Funnel — From Match to Final Authorization Result

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 7.2.2 — least privilege through policy review.

compliance.controlObjective: Least-privilege funnel evidence for ISE authorization-policy decisions.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disable a policy rule (or set conditions impossible). Funnel shows zero match volume after change.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Active policies. Funnel shows expected distribution.

dataSources: ISE syslog *Authorization* — events showing the policy stack: `MatchedPolicyName`, `MatchedRuleName`, `AuthorizationProfile`, `SecurityGroup`, `dACL`, `VLAN`. ISE evaluates policies top-down; the first match wins.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5200, 5236)
| stats count, dc(user) as users by MatchedPolicyName, MatchedRuleName, AuthorizationProfile
| sort -count
description: Tracks how authentications flow through the policy stack. Identifies (a) policy rules that never match (dead rules), (b) catch-all rules that match too often (suggesting more specific rules are missed), (c) authorization profiles that are configured but never assigned.
value: Authorization policies grow over time and accumulate dead rules and catch-all over-grants. Funnel visualization gives policy-engineering teams data-driven input to prune rules and tighten access.
implementation: Schedule the search daily over the last 7 days; review the funnel-drop chart in the ISE Authorization Funnel dashboard.
visualization: Sankey diagram: rules → profiles → SGTs/dACLs/VLANs. Dead-rule list. Catch-all rule top-N.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Newly added rules — match volume builds up over weeks.
(b) Disaster-recovery catch-all rules legitimately match in failure modes.
mitreAttack: N/A (SLO monitoring — measures user enrollment funnel completion, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: When users connect, ISE checks a long list of rules to decide what they're allowed to do. We map out which rules are used and which aren't — so the team can clean up unused rules and tighten access.
